Business Problem: User onboarding completion rate is 35%, well below industry benchmark of 60%. This leads to poor retention and wasted acquisition spend.
Business Outcomes:
• Increase onboarding completion to 60%
  • Improve 30-day retention by 25%
  • Reduce support tickets for "getting started" by 50%
Users:
New users in their first 7 days:

  • Individual contributors exploring the tool
  • Team leads setting up for their group
  • Admins configuring organization settings
Benefits:
• Faster time to value
  • Reduced confusion about next steps
  • Confidence in using core features
Solutions:
• Progressive onboarding checklist
  • Interactive feature tours
  • Personalized setup based on role
  • Quick wins in first session
Hypotheses: We believe that a personalized onboarding checklist will result in higher completion rates. We will know this is true when we see a 20% increase in users completing all onboarding steps within 3 days.
Riskiest Assumption: Users want guidance vs self-exploration. If users prefer to explore on their own, a checklist may feel restrictive and reduce engagement.
Smallest Experiment: A/B test: Show checklist to 50% of new users for 2 weeks. Measure completion rate, time-to-first-action, and 7-day retention. Success: +15% completion with no retention drop.
